new_terms.context: Synonym for thread block clusters or CTA clusters introduced in Hopper.
A kernel design pattern where a fixed number of CTAs run for the duration of the kernel.
The guarantee that CTAs in a cluster are scheduled on the same GPC simultaneously.
A launch attribute in SM100 allowing kernels to request an ideal cluster dimension.
A secondary cluster size used when the preferred size cannot be accommodated due to floorsweeping.
Refers to the idle SMs caused by cluster sizes not evenly dividing the available SMs in a GPC.
